heading: Advanced geothermal computing and data science research and development
label: 17197 Advanced geothermal computing and data science research and development
notes: Amendments2020— amended section generally. Prior to amendment, section related to Center for Geothermal Technology Transfer.Pub. L. 116–260
Effective DateSection effective on the date that is 1 day after , see , set out as a note under , The Congress.Dec. 19, 2007section 1601 of Pub. L. 110–140section 1824 of Title 2
amendments: Amendments2020— amended section generally. Prior to amendment, section related to Center for Geothermal Technology Transfer.Pub. L. 116–260

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Secretary shall carry out a program of research and development of advanced computing and data science tools for geothermal energy.

children.heading: Advanced computing for geothermal systems technologies
children.label: 1 Advanced computing for geothermal systems technologies
children.text: Research, development, and demonstration of technologies to develop advanced data, machine learning, artificial intelligence, and related computing tools to assist in locating geothermal resources, to increase the reliability of site characterization, to increase the rate and efficiency of drilling, to improve induced seismicity mitigation, and to support enhanced geothermal systems technologies.

children.heading: Geothermal systems reservoir modeling
children.label: 2 Geothermal systems reservoir modeling
children.text: Research, development, and demonstration of models of geothermal reservoir performance and enhanced geothermal systems reservoir stimulation technologies and techniques, with an emphasis on accurately modeling fluid and heat flow, permeability evolution, geomechanics, geochemistry, seismicity, and operational performance over time, including collaboration with industry and field validation.

children.heading: Coordination
children.label: c Coordination
children.text: In carrying out these programs, the Secretary shall ensure coordination and consultation with the Department of Energy’s Office of Science. The Secretary shall ensure, to the maximum extent practicable, coordination of these activities with the Department of Energy National Laboratories, institutes of higher education, and the private sector.
